interactions.summary: Jiang concludes the symbol section by saying art combines imagination into society, belonging, and community.

A student asks whether humans were trying to connect with divine spirits; Jiang clarifies that the divine is already all around us, so ritual is not connection to a distant realm but harmonization with an already unified material-spiritual world.

Jiang compares cave paintings to contemporary street art and Inuit art, arguing that art gives meaning to communities and unifies common imagination; preliterate people were more intuitive, imaginative, and empathic.

claims.claim: Art is presented as a social technology for combining imagination into society, belonging, and community.
Jiang clarifies that humans do not connect to a separate divine spirit; they already coexist with the divine and use ritual to harmonize material life with the spirit world.
claims.temporal_scope: answer to student in this lecture

claims.claim: He says preliterate people were more intuitive, imaginative, and empathic than modern literate people, able to perceive emotions without asking.

signature_moments.moment: The divine is not elsewhere; the world is already one material-spiritual field, and ritual reconciles human acts within it.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This corrects a possible misunderstanding and anchors the episode's theology of immanence.

signature_moments.moment: Street art becomes modern cave painting: a community telling itself where it came from and where it is going.
signature_moments.source_phrase: No different from the cave paintings
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It carries the Ice Age model into contemporary public art without needing a separate lens pass.

signature_moments.moment: Empathy is immediate perception: Jiang says he does not need to ask how you feel because he can know right away.
signature_moments.source_phrase: I know exactly how you're feeling right away
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This sets up the later horse, autism, mother-child, and social-media examples.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: A shared imaginative field built by art, story, ritual, and symbols to bind a community.
Not reaching a separate spirit world but reconciling human acts with an already divine world.
